title: Microsoft Identity and Access Administrator
summary: Identity lifecycle, secure access, workload identities, and governance for Microsoft Entra environments, with multi-tenant administration scenarios.

questions.prompt: An MSP help-desk team must reset passwords only for users in one customer division, without receiving tenant-wide user permissions. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: An administrative unit scopes a supported Microsoft Entra role assignment to the objects in that unit. A normal group does not scope an administrative role.
questions.sourceName: Administrative units in Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: An administrative unit containing the division users, with a scoped Helpdesk Administrator assignment
A security group containing the division users, with a tenant-wide Helpdesk Administrator assignment
A Microsoft 365 group containing the division users, with group ownership assigned to the MSP
A catalog containing the division users, with the MSP added as a catalog owner

questions.prompt: A tenant must automatically license all enabled employees whose department is Support. Which design requires the least recurring administration?
questions.explanation: A dynamic user group can evaluate user attributes automatically, and group-based licensing applies the selected licenses to current group members.
questions.sourceName: Group-based licensing in Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: Use a dynamic user group based on department and account status, then assign the license to the group
Use an assigned security group and schedule an access review to add newly hired employees
Use an administrative unit based on department and assign the license to the unit
Use a dynamic device group based on device ownership and assign the user license to the group

questions.prompt: A provider needs to tag service accounts with a controlled CustomerCode value. Help-desk administrators must not be able to read or change the tag merely because they can manage users. Which feature best provides this separation?
questions.explanation: Custom security attributes use separate attribute-definition and assignment roles. Their values are not automatically exposed through ordinary user-management permissions.

choices.text: A custom security attribute set with dedicated attribute roles
A directory extension with the User Administrator role
A dynamic group rule with the Groups Administrator role
An administrative unit with the Directory Readers role

questions.prompt: Users from a trusted partner tenant already satisfy MFA on their home tenant. Your tenant should accept that MFA claim when they access your applications, while your tenant remains responsible for authorization. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Inbound cross-tenant access trust settings can trust MFA claims from a specified external tenant. Resource-tenant Conditional Access and authorization still apply.

choices.text: Inbound cross-tenant trust settings for multifactor authentication
Outbound cross-tenant access settings that trust compliant devices
Tenant restrictions that allow the partner tenant
Cross-tenant synchronization with automatic redemption enabled

questions.prompt: Customer A must provision selected internal users as B2B collaboration users in Customer B and remove access when those users leave the assigned source group. Where is the cross-tenant synchronization configuration created?
questions.explanation: Cross-tenant synchronization is configured in the source tenant. It uses the target tenant's cross-tenant access consent and provisions scoped users into the target tenant.

choices.text: In Customer A, with Customer B configured as the target tenant
In Customer B, with Customer A configured as the target tenant
In both tenants, each configured as the other's target tenant
Only in the Microsoft 365 admin center of Customer B

questions.prompt: A customer has several disconnected AD DS forests and wants a lightweight cloud-managed synchronization configuration with multiple agents for availability. Which synchronization technology best matches the requirement?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync uses lightweight provisioning agents and cloud-managed configuration. Multiple active agents can improve availability and support disconnected forests.
questions.sourceName: What is Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync?

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync
Microsoft Entra Connect Sync staging mode
Microsoft Entra cross-tenant synchronization
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy

questions.prompt: A customer is removing AD FS. They want cloud authentication that continues when on-premises authentication infrastructure is unavailable and supports leaked-credential detection for synchronized users. Which primary sign-in method should you recommend?
questions.explanation: Password hash synchronization supports cloud authentication without an on-premises authentication agent in the sign-in path and enables capabilities that depend on cloud password-hash data, such as leaked-credential detection.

choices.text: Password hash synchronization
Pass-through authentication without password hash synchronization
Federation with a second AD FS farm
Seamless single sign-on as the authentication method

questions.prompt: A remote new starter has no registered authentication method. The user must securely register a passkey without receiving a permanent password from the help desk. What should an administrator issue?
questions.explanation: A Temporary Access Pass is a time-limited credential that can bootstrap passwordless authentication method registration and device setup.

choices.text: A time-limited Temporary Access Pass
A one-time bypass for multifactor authentication
A password reset registration campaign
A certificate-based authentication binding

questions.prompt: Only phishing-resistant authentication must be accepted for privileged portal access. Users may use FIDO2 security keys or certificate-based authentication. Which Conditional Access control should you select?
questions.explanation: Authentication strengths let Conditional Access require a defined set of method combinations. The built-in phishing-resistant strength includes supported phishing-resistant methods rather than accepting any MFA method.

choices.text: Require the phishing-resistant MFA authentication strength
Require multifactor authentication
Require a compliant device
Require an approved client app

questions.prompt: An MSP is preparing a Conditional Access policy that would block legacy authentication in a customer tenant. The team needs evidence of likely impact before enforcement. Which policy state should be used first?
questions.explanation: Report-only mode evaluates a Conditional Access policy and records the result without enforcing its controls. Sign-in logs can then be reviewed for impact.

choices.text: Report-only
On with the What If tool enabled
Off with sign-in diagnostics enabled
On with session controls omitted

questions.prompt: You are deploying a baseline Conditional Access policy to all users. Which accounts should normally be explicitly excluded to reduce tenant lockout risk?
questions.explanation: Emergency access accounts should be excluded from Conditional Access policies so administrators retain a recovery path if normal authentication or policy configuration fails.

choices.text: Dedicated emergency access accounts
All accounts assigned eligible roles in PIM
All guest accounts from trusted tenants
All accounts synchronized from AD DS

questions.prompt: A Conditional Access policy must let a user safely remediate a high user-risk detection without help-desk action. Which access control is appropriate?
questions.explanation: A user-risk policy can require a secure password change. Successful remediation changes the password and addresses the user-risk state; sign-in risk is normally remediated with MFA.

choices.text: Require a secure password change
Require an approved client app
Require terms of use
Require a Microsoft Entra hybrid joined device

questions.prompt: An administrator disables a compromised user and needs supported applications to react to the critical event without waiting for the normal access-token lifetime. Which capability addresses this?
questions.explanation: Continuous access evaluation allows supported resource providers and clients to respond quickly to critical events such as account disablement and session revocation.

choices.text: Continuous access evaluation
Sign-in frequency session control
Persistent browser session control
Token protection session control

questions.prompt: Remote users need identity-aware access to a private on-premises TCP application without exposing it publicly or granting broad network access. Which Microsoft Entra capability best fits?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Private Access, part of Global Secure Access, provides identity-centric access to private applications and resources using configured connectors and app segments.
questions.sourceName: What is Microsoft Entra Private Access?

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Private Access
Microsoft Entra Internet Access for Microsoft 365
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy with header-based authentication
Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps Cloud Discovery

questions.prompt: A hybrid customer wants the Microsoft global banned-password list and a tenant-specific banned list enforced when users change passwords on on-premises domain controllers. Which two components are required on-premises?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Password Protection for AD DS uses proxy services and DC agents. The proxy communicates with Microsoft Entra, while DC agents enforce policy on domain controllers.
questions.sourceName: Enforce Microsoft Entra Password Protection on-premises

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Password Protection proxy service
Microsoft Entra Password Protection DC agents
Microsoft Entra Connect Health agent for Sync
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy connector

questions.prompt: Three Azure VMs must share one workload identity, and that identity must remain available if any VM is deleted. Which identity type should be used?
questions.explanation: A user-assigned managed identity has an independent lifecycle and can be assigned to multiple Azure resources. A system-assigned identity is tied to one resource.

choices.text: A user-assigned managed identity
A system-assigned managed identity on the first VM
A service principal created from each VM
A synchronized service account with password hash synchronization

questions.prompt: An Azure Function needs to read secrets from Azure Key Vault. The solution must avoid storing application credentials and should remove the identity when the function is deleted. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: A system-assigned managed identity is tied to the Azure resource lifecycle and obtains tokens without application-managed secrets. It can be granted the required Key Vault role.

choices.text: Enable a system-assigned managed identity on the function and grant it Key Vault access
Register an application and store its client secret in the function settings
Create a user-assigned identity and store its object ID as a function secret
Create a managed service account in AD DS and synchronize it to Microsoft Entra ID

questions.prompt: An internal web application must be published through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy. The design must tolerate one connector server failing. What should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Deploying at least two connectors in the same connector group provides resilience and load distribution for applications assigned to that group.
questions.sourceName: Application Proxy connectors and connector groups

choices.text: At least two connectors in the same connector group
One connector in each of two unrelated connector groups
One connector and one Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync agent
Two enterprise applications using the same external URL

questions.prompt: Users are blocked from granting consent to high-impact applications, but they need a governed way to request review by designated administrators. Which feature should you configure?
questions.explanation: The admin consent workflow lets users submit consent requests for applications that require administrator approval and routes them to configured reviewers.

choices.text: Admin consent workflow
Application assignment required
Entitlement management access package approval
Privileged Identity Management approval

questions.prompt: A daemon service runs without a signed-in user and must read directory data through Microsoft Graph. Which permission type is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Application permissions are used by apps that call an API as themselves without a signed-in user. Delegated permissions act within a signed-in user's context.
questions.sourceName: Permissions and consent in Microsoft identity platform

choices.text: An application permission granted to the app registration
A delegated permission consented by any signed-in user
An app role assigned to the service's managed device
A Microsoft Entra directory role assigned to the API itself

questions.prompt: A SaaS application exposes Approver and Reader roles. The MSP wants access controlled through Microsoft Entra groups and the role claim emitted to the application. What should be assigned?
questions.explanation: App roles defined by the application can be assigned to users or groups on the enterprise application. Microsoft Entra includes the assigned roles in tokens for the application.
questions.sourceName: Add app roles to an application

choices.text: Assign each Microsoft Entra group to the corresponding app role on the enterprise application
Assign each group a Microsoft Entra directory role with the same display name
Add each group to an administrative unit named for the application role
Grant each group delegated API permissions on the app registration

questions.prompt: Users may access SharePoint Online from unmanaged devices, but downloads containing sensitive data must be blocked during the session. Which integrated control should you use?
questions.explanation: Conditional Access App Control routes supported sessions through Defender for Cloud Apps, where a session policy can inspect and control activities such as downloads.

choices.text: Conditional Access App Control with a Defender for Cloud Apps session policy
A Defender for Cloud Apps activity policy without session proxying
A Conditional Access sign-in frequency session control
An entitlement management access package with a terms-of-use policy

questions.prompt: A customer wants employees of a partner company to request a package containing a group, an application, and a SharePoint site. Requests must be limited to that partner's users. What should be added to the catalog policy?
questions.explanation: A connected organization represents an external directory or domain for entitlement management. An access package policy can restrict requests to users from that connected organization.

choices.text: A connected organization for the partner, referenced by the access package policy
An administrative unit for the partner, referenced by the catalog
A cross-tenant access default setting, referenced by the access review
A connector group for the partner, referenced by the enterprise application

questions.prompt: External users should lose package resources when their assignment expires. If they have no other access package assignments, their guest accounts should later be removed automatically. Which feature controls this account cleanup?
questions.explanation: Entitlement management external-user lifecycle settings can block sign-in and remove external user accounts after the last access package assignment is lost, using a configured delay.
questions.sourceName: Manage external user lifecycle in entitlement management

choices.text: Entitlement management external-user lifecycle settings
Cross-tenant synchronization accidental-deletion thresholds
Conditional Access sign-in frequency
PIM assignment expiration for the Guest Inviter role

questions.prompt: A customer must regularly verify guest membership across many Microsoft 365 groups and automatically remove access when reviewers do not respond. Which configuration best meets the requirement?
questions.explanation: A recurring access review can target guest users across groups and apply a configured result when reviewers do not respond. Auto-apply can remove denied access at completion.
questions.sourceName: Create an access review of groups and applications

choices.text: A recurring access review scoped to guest users, with auto-apply and a deny-on-no-response fallback
A recurring PIM access review of eligible role assignments, with approval required
A recurring Conditional Access report-only evaluation, with session revocation
A recurring cross-tenant synchronization job, with automatic redemption disabled

questions.prompt: Support engineers should hold no standing Privileged Role Administrator access. When needed, they must provide justification, complete MFA, and obtain approval for a limited period. Which configuration should you use?
questions.explanation: PIM eligible assignments provide just-in-time role activation. Role settings can require MFA, justification, approval, and limit activation duration.
questions.sourceName: Microsoft Entra roles in Privileged Identity Management

choices.text: An eligible PIM role assignment with activation approval, MFA, justification, and duration settings
An active time-bound role assignment with an access review and no activation settings
A scoped administrative-unit assignment with self-service password reset
An access package assignment containing the role and a recurring request policy

questions.prompt: Membership in a security group grants access to multiple sensitive applications. Members should activate group membership only when needed, subject to approval. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: PIM for Groups can make group membership or ownership eligible and enforce activation controls such as approval, MFA, and time limits.

choices.text: PIM for Groups with eligible member assignments
PIM for Microsoft Entra roles with the group assigned as role eligible
An access review that automatically adds approved users to the group
A dynamic membership rule based on users' authentication strength

questions.prompt: Which two practices are appropriate when creating emergency access accounts for a Microsoft Entra tenant?
questions.explanation: Emergency accounts should avoid dependencies that could fail with normal administrative access. Microsoft recommends cloud-only emergency accounts and monitoring their sign-ins because usage should be exceptional.

choices.text: Use cloud-only accounts that do not depend on on-premises federation or synchronization
Configure monitoring and alerts for any sign-in activity
Assign the accounts to the same authentication dependencies as daily administrators
Use the accounts for scheduled administration so their credentials remain familiar

questions.prompt: An MSP wants centralized KQL investigation of Microsoft Entra sign-in logs and also needs to stream the same category of logs to a customer SIEM. Which two diagnostic destinations should be configured?
questions.explanation: A Log Analytics workspace supports KQL analysis, while Azure Event Hubs provides event streaming to external systems. Microsoft Entra diagnostic settings can route selected log categories to both.

choices.text: A Log Analytics workspace
An Azure Event Hubs namespace
A Microsoft Entra access package catalog
A Microsoft Entra Application Proxy connector group

questions.prompt: An administrator needs to determine who changed a Conditional Access policy yesterday and which properties were modified. Which Microsoft Entra log should be examined first?
questions.explanation: Audit logs record changes to Microsoft Entra resources, including the actor, target resource, activity, status, and modified properties. Sign-in logs record authentication activity instead.

choices.text: Audit logs
Interactive user sign-in logs
Provisioning logs
Risky workload identities

questions.prompt: A provider needs a tenant-level prioritized view of identity security improvements, with points that can be tracked over time. Which Microsoft Entra report should be used?
questions.explanation: Identity Secure Score measures alignment with Microsoft's identity security recommendations and provides improvement actions and score history.

choices.text: Identity Secure Score
Microsoft 365 Apps usage report
Microsoft Entra provisioning insights
Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps Cloud Discovery score

questions.prompt: An administrator receives a Microsoft Entra role through a role-assignable group and another role scoped to an administrative unit. Which two factors should be evaluated when determining whether the administrator can update a specific user?
questions.explanation: Effective access depends on permissions from direct and group-based role assignments and on the scope of each assignment. A role at administrative-unit scope applies only to supported objects in that unit.

choices.text: The permissions included in all applicable direct and group-based role assignments
The scope of each applicable role assignment
The user's license assignment source
The user's most recent authentication strength

questions.prompt: A tenant must allow B2B invitations to users from two approved partner domains but prevent invitations to other external domains. Which setting directly controls this?
questions.explanation: External collaboration settings include collaboration restrictions that can allow invitations only to specified domains or deny invitations to specified domains.

choices.text: B2B collaboration restrictions configured as an allowlist of the partner domains
Inbound cross-tenant access defaults configured to trust partner MFA
A Conditional Access named location containing partner DNS servers
An entitlement management catalog restricted to external sponsors

questions.prompt: Changing a high-impact tenant setting must trigger phishing-resistant authentication even when the administrator already has an active session. Which design should you implement?
questions.explanation: Protected actions associate supported permissions with a Conditional Access authentication context. A policy targeting that context can require an authentication strength when the protected operation is attempted.
questions.sourceName: Protected actions in Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: Associate the action with an authentication context and target that context with Conditional Access
Set sign-in frequency to Every time for every cloud application
Make the administrator eligible for the role in PIM without changing activation settings
Configure continuous access evaluation as disabled for the administrator

questions.prompt: A customer wants users who authenticate with older MFA methods to be prompted during sign-in to register Microsoft Authenticator, while excluding a pilot exception group. Which feature should be configured?
questions.explanation: An authentication methods registration campaign prompts selected users to set up Microsoft Authenticator and supports included and excluded groups.

choices.text: An authentication methods registration campaign
A self-service password reset registration policy
A Conditional Access report-only policy
A Temporary Access Pass policy targeted to all users

questions.prompt: A Conditional Access policy applies to all devices, but must exclude only company-managed emergency workstations identified by a stable device attribute. Group membership changes are too slow for the requirement. What should you use?
questions.explanation: A Conditional Access filter for devices evaluates supported device properties during policy processing and can include or exclude matching devices without relying on group membership evaluation.

choices.text: An exclude filter for devices using the stable device attribute
An excluded dynamic device group evaluated during every token request
An excluded named location based on the device attribute
An authentication context assigned to the device object

questions.prompt: Users may consent only to low-impact delegated permissions from applications published by organizations Microsoft has verified. Which tenant setting should be configured?
questions.explanation: The user consent policy can restrict consent to selected low-impact permissions for apps from verified publishers, reducing risk while retaining limited self-service consent.

choices.text: Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions
Allow user consent for apps from any publisher, for all delegated permissions
Disable user consent and assign the Application Administrator role to all users
Enable admin consent workflow and automatically approve every verified publisher

questions.prompt: An on-premises web app is published through Application Proxy. The tenant wants Conditional Access evaluated before traffic reaches the connector. Which preauthentication method should be selected?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra preauthentication requires users to authenticate with Microsoft Entra ID before Application Proxy forwards traffic, enabling Conditional Access enforcement.

choices.text: Microsoft Entra ID preauthentication
Passthrough preauthentication
Header-based sign-on without Microsoft Entra preauthentication
Integrated Windows authentication performed before Application Proxy

questions.prompt: A security team wants Defender for Cloud Apps to alert when an OAuth app requests high-privilege access and to revoke a risky app's permissions after investigation. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: OAuth app policies in Defender for Cloud Apps evaluate OAuth app characteristics and permissions, generate alerts, and support governance actions such as revoking an app.
questions.sourceName: OAuth app policies in Defender for Cloud Apps

choices.text: An OAuth app policy with the appropriate governance action
A Cloud Discovery anomaly policy with an endpoint isolation action
A Conditional Access session policy with a password reset action
An access package policy with automatic assignment

questions.prompt: External contractors must accept the current acceptable-use agreement before receiving an access package assignment, and acceptance records must be reviewable. Which feature should be integrated into the policy?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra terms of use can require acceptance and record acceptance history. Terms can be required through Conditional Access and used with entitlement management scenarios.

choices.text: Microsoft Entra terms of use
An access review decision helper
A PIM activation justification
A custom security attribute assignment

questions.prompt: Two customer tenants want users to collaborate in Teams shared channels while each user remains represented by an identity in the home tenant rather than a B2B guest object in the resource tenant. Which External ID method fits?
questions.explanation: B2B direct connect enables reciprocal cross-tenant access to supported shared channels without creating ordinary B2B guest accounts. Both organizations configure cross-tenant access settings.

choices.text: B2B collaboration with automatic redemption
B2B direct connect with reciprocal cross-tenant access settings
Cross-tenant synchronization in both directions
A multitenant organization with member synchronization disabled

questions.prompt: A consultant signs in to Microsoft 365 from a personally owned Windows laptop. The organization needs a device identity for Conditional Access, but the consultant must continue signing in to Windows with a personal account. Which device state is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra registration is designed for personally owned devices and adds an organizational device identity without changing the user's Windows sign-in to an organizational account.

choices.text: Microsoft Entra hybrid joined
Microsoft Entra joined
Microsoft Entra registered
AD DS registered through Seamless SSO

questions.prompt: An MSP must place a customer's logo, help-desk text, and privacy link on Microsoft Entra sign-in pages without changing any application. What should be configured?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra company branding customizes organization-specific sign-in experiences, including visual elements and support or privacy links. It does not require per-application changes.

choices.text: A custom authentication context
An enterprise application home page
A terms-of-use document
Microsoft Entra company branding

questions.prompt: A customer must invite 400 partner users from a supplied list and retain a repeatable record of each invitation address and redirect URL. Which approach best fits the requirement?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra bulk invitation accepts a CSV based on the downloadable template and creates B2B invitations in one operation. It is more appropriate than manual invitations or cross-tenant synchronization for a one-time supplied list.

choices.text: Upload a completed bulk-invite CSV in Microsoft Entra ID
Create a dynamic group whose rule contains the external email addresses
Configure cross-tenant synchronization without consent in the target tenant
Add the addresses to an administrative unit and enable self-service sign-up

questions.prompt: Group-based licensing reports that a newly created cloud user cannot receive a Microsoft 365 license, although licenses remain available. The user's country is present, but Usage location is blank. What should be corrected?
questions.explanation: A user's Usage location determines which licensed services can legally be made available and must be set before a license can be assigned. The general country or region profile value is not a substitute.
questions.sourceName: Assign Microsoft 365 licenses to user accounts

choices.text: Set the tenant's preferred data location
Set the user's Usage location, then reprocess the license assignment
Convert the licensing group to a Microsoft 365 group
Add the user to an administrative unit in the same geography

questions.prompt: A customer wants a second Microsoft Entra Connect Sync server ready for controlled failover. It should import and synchronize data but must not export changes while the primary server is active. Which mode should be enabled on the second server?
questions.explanation: A Connect Sync server in staging mode performs imports and synchronization but suppresses exports and password synchronization. An administrator can compare results and deliberately switch the active server during failover.
questions.sourceName: Microsoft Entra Connect Sync operational tasks

choices.text: Pass-through authentication mode
Seamless SSO mode
Staging mode
Soft-match quarantine mode

questions.prompt: A tenant requires ordinary users to prove identity with one registered method for self-service password reset, but administrators must use two methods. Which configuration supports this distinction?
questions.explanation: SSPR authentication-method settings define the number of methods required for reset. Administrator accounts are subject to the stricter administrator reset policy and must use two authentication methods.

choices.text: Set one method for SSPR; the administrator reset policy still requires two methods
Create separate authentication strengths for users and administrators
Set two methods for SSPR and exempt users through a named location
Enable password writeback only for administrator accounts

questions.prompt: Hybrid-joined Windows users need passwordless Windows Hello for Business access to on-premises Kerberos resources. The customer wants the simplest supported deployment and does not require certificate authentication. Which trust model should be selected?
questions.explanation: Cloud Kerberos trust is the recommended simplified Windows Hello for Business trust model for hybrid environments when certificate authentication is not required. It avoids deploying user certificates for this scenario.
questions.sourceName: Windows Hello for Business cloud Kerberos trust

choices.text: Certificate trust with user authentication certificates
Key trust with AD FS as a registration authority
Pass-through authentication with Seamless SSO
Cloud Kerberos trust

questions.prompt: A customer enables Microsoft Entra certificate-based authentication. Certificates contain a reliable certificate policy object identifier that should determine whether a certificate satisfies single-factor or multifactor authentication. What should be configured?
questions.explanation: The certificate-based authentication policy uses authentication binding rules to map certificate attributes, such as policy OIDs, to an authentication strength. Username bindings instead determine which user the certificate identifies.

choices.text: A username binding from Subject to userPrincipalName
An authentication binding rule for the certificate policy OID
A Conditional Access device filter for the issuing CA
A custom security attribute on the certificate authority

questions.prompt: Before changing policy state, an administrator wants to simulate which Conditional Access policies would apply to one user accessing Exchange Online from a specific location and device platform. Which tool should be used?
questions.explanation: The Conditional Access What If tool evaluates a supplied sign-in scenario and reports policies that would apply, would not apply, or cannot be evaluated. It does not create an actual sign-in.

choices.text: Sign-in diagnostic on an unrelated successful sign-in
Identity Secure Score
The Conditional Access What If tool
A provisioning-on-demand test

questions.prompt: Administrators may keep browser sessions, but must be required to reauthenticate for a privileged cloud application every four hours. Which Conditional Access session control directly sets that interval?
questions.explanation: The sign-in frequency session control defines how often a user must reauthenticate for targeted resources. Persistent browser controls affect whether a browser session survives closure, not the four-hour reauthentication interval.

choices.text: Continuous access evaluation strict location enforcement
A persistent browser session set to Never persistent
Token protection for sign-in sessions
Sign-in frequency set to four hours

questions.prompt: An investigation confirms that an account is compromised, but Microsoft Entra ID Protection currently shows the user risk as low. Which action records the finding and raises the user's risk state?
questions.explanation: Confirm user compromised records the administrator's conclusion and moves the user risk to high. Dismiss user risk marks the user safe and is inappropriate when compromise is confirmed.

choices.text: Dismiss user risk
Confirm user compromised
Confirm sign-in safe
Add the user to a trusted named location

questions.prompt: Remote endpoints must send general internet traffic, not only Microsoft 365 traffic, through Global Secure Access for web filtering and identity-aware policy. Which traffic-forwarding profile is required?
questions.explanation: The Internet traffic forwarding profile acquires general internet traffic for Microsoft Entra Internet Access. The Microsoft 365 profile is limited to Microsoft 365 traffic, and Private Access handles private resources.

choices.text: Private access profile
Microsoft 365 traffic profile
Internet traffic profile
Cross-tenant access profile

questions.prompt: A multitenant SaaS application's definition is registered in its publisher tenant. When a customer administrator grants consent in another tenant, which object is created there to represent that application instance?
questions.explanation: An app registration creates an application object in the home tenant. Consent in another tenant creates a service principal, also shown as an enterprise application, that represents the local instance and assignments.

choices.text: A managed identity tied to the customer tenant
A second application object owned by the customer
A service principal in the customer tenant
A cross-tenant synchronization configuration

questions.prompt: A GitHub Actions workflow must authenticate as a Microsoft Entra application without storing a client secret in GitHub. Which configuration provides the required trust?
questions.explanation: A federated identity credential establishes trust between the application and tokens from an external OpenID Connect issuer such as GitHub. The workflow exchanges its token for a Microsoft identity platform token without a stored client secret.

choices.text: A federated identity credential matching the GitHub issuer, subject, and audience
A delegated permission with offline_access granted to the workflow
A user-assigned managed identity attached directly to the GitHub runner service
A certificate credential exported to a repository variable

questions.prompt: A SaaS application supports SCIM 2.0. Assigned users must be created, updated, and disabled automatically in the SaaS directory according to Microsoft Entra assignments. Which enterprise-application feature should be configured?
questions.explanation: Automatic application provisioning uses SCIM to synchronize assigned identities and lifecycle changes to supported SaaS applications. SSO alone authenticates users but does not provision their accounts.
questions.sourceName: Automate user provisioning to SaaS applications

choices.text: SAML single sign-on with just-in-time claims only
Application Proxy connector-based provisioning
Cross-tenant synchronization to the SaaS vendor tenant
Automatic provisioning with the application's SCIM endpoint and credentials

questions.prompt: An on-premises web application uses Integrated Windows Authentication. Users should authenticate to Microsoft Entra ID externally and receive SSO to the application without entering the AD DS password again. Which Application Proxy SSO method fits?
questions.explanation: Application Proxy can use Kerberos constrained delegation for Integrated Windows Authentication. The connector obtains a Kerberos token on behalf of the user after Microsoft Entra preauthentication.
questions.sourceName: Kerberos constrained delegation for Application Proxy

choices.text: Password-based SSO with credentials stored in the browser
Kerberos constrained delegation
Linked sign-on to the internal URL
SAML token translation performed by Cloud Sync

questions.prompt: An enterprise application should allow sign-in only for users and groups explicitly assigned to it. The application itself does not enforce its own assignment list. Which property should be enabled?
questions.explanation: When Assignment required is enabled on an enterprise application, Microsoft Entra ID permits user sign-in only when the user or an applicable group is assigned. Consent and app-role definitions solve different problems.
questions.sourceName: Manage user and group assignment to an application

choices.text: Visible to users
User assignment required
Enabled for users to sign in
Admin consent requests enabled

questions.prompt: Identity Protection detects high risk for a service principal used by an automated integration. The organization wants Conditional Access to stop that workload identity without attempting MFA. Which policy design is appropriate?
questions.explanation: Conditional Access for workload identities can target service principals and block access when service-principal risk is high. Workload identities cannot satisfy user grant controls such as MFA or compliant device.

choices.text: A user-risk policy requiring secure password change
A workload-identity policy requiring phishing-resistant MFA
A workload-identity policy that blocks access at high service-principal risk
A sign-in-risk policy requiring a compliant device

questions.prompt: New employees should automatically receive a Temporary Access Pass and a welcome email a few days before the employeeHireDate attribute. Which governance capability should orchestrate these time-based tasks?
questions.explanation: Lifecycle Workflows automate joiner, mover, and leaver tasks based on identity attributes and time-based triggers. Built-in tasks include generating a Temporary Access Pass and sending email.

choices.text: A recurring access review of new employees
A Lifecycle Workflow using an employeeHireDate-based trigger
A dynamic group with a PIM activation rule
An access package with an automatic assignment policy only

questions.prompt: A catalog already contains the correct group and application resources. The customer now needs different approval, expiration, and requestor rules for employees and partner users. Which entitlement-management object should be created for each population?
questions.explanation: An access package assignment policy defines who can request or receive the package, approval behavior, assignment duration, and lifecycle rules. The catalog and resource roles define what access the package contains.

choices.text: A separate catalog for each population
A separate connected organization for every employee
A separate access package assignment policy for each population
A separate administrative unit for each approval stage

questions.prompt: Each application access group has a different business owner. A quarterly review should route membership decisions to the owner of the group being reviewed without maintaining a central reviewer list. Who should be selected as reviewer?
questions.explanation: Access reviews can use group owners as reviewers, which routes each group's decisions to its current owners. Selected users or users reviewing their own access would not meet the decentralized ownership requirement.
questions.sourceName: Create an access review of groups or applications

choices.text: Members review their own access
The tenant's Global Readers
The access package catalog owners
Group owners

questions.prompt: An engineer needs just-in-time Owner access to one Azure subscription, not an administrative role in Microsoft Entra ID. Which PIM area should the administrator manage?
questions.explanation: Owner is an Azure RBAC role. PIM for Azure resources manages eligible and active assignments at management-group, subscription, resource-group, or resource scope; PIM for Microsoft Entra roles manages directory roles.

choices.text: PIM for Groups at tenant scope
PIM for Azure resources at the subscription scope
PIM for Microsoft Entra roles at administrative-unit scope
Entitlement management for connected organizations

questions.prompt: Users assigned the Accounts-Payable-Approver package must not receive the Accounts-Payable-Requester package. Which entitlement-management control should be configured on the requester package?
questions.explanation: Entitlement management separation-of-duties checks can mark another access package or group as incompatible. A request is then prevented when the user already has the conflicting access.

choices.text: A Conditional Access authentication context
An incompatible access package for separation of duties
A catalog resource role with hidden membership
An access review with recommendations disabled

questions.prompt: A leaver workflow must call an MSP-owned ticketing API after disabling a user's access. The required action is not a built-in Lifecycle Workflows task. What should be added?
questions.explanation: A Lifecycle Workflows custom task extension can invoke an Azure Logic Apps workflow, allowing the lifecycle workflow to integrate with an external system while retaining workflow execution history.

choices.text: A custom task extension backed by Azure Logic Apps
A custom security attribute definition backed by Microsoft Graph
A Conditional Access custom control
A PIM alert routed directly to the ticketing API

questions.prompt: An MSP must let a customer's project contractors request access to an application and a SharePoint site with manager approval and automatic expiration. None of these resources is in entitlement management yet. Which build order is correct?
questions.explanation: Resources must first be available in a catalog. The access package can then be created with the required resource roles, followed by an assignment policy that defines requestors, approval, and expiration. Requests can be tested only after those dependent objects exist. Creating a policy before its package or adding resource roles before cataloging the resources reverses those dependencies.
questions.sourceName: Create and manage an entitlement management catalog

choices.text: Create the catalog; add the application and site resources; create the access package with resource roles; add the approval and expiration policy; test a request
Create the approval policy; create the access package; add resources to a catalog; add resource roles; test a request
Add resource roles to an access package; create the catalog; add resources; create the policy; test a request
Create the catalog; create the assignment policy; add resources; test a request; create the access package
Create the catalog; add resources; create the policy without a package; test a request; then add resource roles

questions.prompt: A cloud-only customer is piloting passkeys for users who have no usable MFA method. Change control requires the passkey method to be enabled for the pilot before any bootstrap credential is issued. The identity team will then use a Temporary Access Pass. Which sequence is correct?
questions.explanation: The tenant must first enable and target the passkey authentication method. An authorized administrator can then issue a Temporary Access Pass, which the user uses for strong bootstrap authentication before registering the passkey in Security info. Testing passkey sign-in comes last. A user cannot register a method that is not enabled for them, and the passkey cannot be used before registration.
questions.sourceName: Register a passkey with Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: Enable and target passkeys; issue a Temporary Access Pass; have the user authenticate with the pass; register the passkey; test passkey sign-in
Issue a Temporary Access Pass; register the passkey; enable passkeys; authenticate with the pass; test sign-in
Register the passkey; enable passkeys; issue a Temporary Access Pass; test sign-in; authenticate with the pass
Enable passkeys; test passkey sign-in; issue a Temporary Access Pass; register the passkey; authenticate with the pass
Enable passkeys; issue a Temporary Access Pass; register the passkey before authenticating; test sign-in
Issue a Temporary Access Pass; enable passkeys after the user signs in; test sign-in; register the passkey

questions.prompt: A resource tenant's Conditional Access policies require MFA and a compliant device. A trusted partner already enforces both controls in its home tenant, and the resource tenant should accept those results for B2B users instead of requiring duplicate registration. Choose two inbound trust settings to enable for that partner.
questions.explanation: Inbound cross-tenant access settings can trust the partner tenant's MFA claim and its compliant-device claim so those claims satisfy corresponding Conditional Access controls in the resource tenant. B2B direct connect and automatic redemption change collaboration or consent behavior, not whether MFA and device-compliance claims are trusted.

choices.text: Trust multifactor authentication from the partner's Microsoft Entra tenant
Trust compliant-device claims from the partner's Microsoft Entra tenant
Enable B2B direct connect for every external application
Trust Microsoft Entra hybrid join claims instead of compliant-device claims
Enable automatic redemption as a replacement for Conditional Access trust

questions.prompt: A federated customer is redesigning emergency access so administrators can recover from an identity-provider outage or Conditional Access lockout. Choose three controls that Microsoft recommends for these accounts.
questions.explanation: Microsoft recommends at least two cloud-only emergency accounts, permanent active Global Administrator assignment, and monitoring that alerts on their rare use. Synchronizing the accounts preserves the federation dependency, PIM eligibility can leave no active approver during an outage, and routine shared-mailbox use conflicts with emergency-only operation.

choices.text: Maintain at least two cloud-only accounts using the tenant's onmicrosoft.com domain
Keep the Global Administrator role permanently active rather than merely PIM-eligible
Alert on sign-in and audit activity involving the emergency accounts
Synchronize the accounts from on-premises Active Directory to centralize password policy
Make all emergency role assignments eligible and require approval from another eligible administrator
Use the accounts routinely as shared mailboxes so inactivity controls do not remove them

questions.prompt: A customer has tenant-wide Helpdesk Administrators, but only a dedicated executive-support team may reset executive passwords or modify executive security groups. The restriction must apply even against normally authorized tenant-level administrators. What should contain the executive objects?
questions.explanation: A restricted management administrative unit protects supported member objects from modification by administrators outside the restricted unit, even if they hold relevant tenant-wide roles. A normal administrative unit scopes delegated permissions but does not impose this stronger protection, and a dynamic group does not create an administrative boundary.

choices.text: A restricted management administrative unit
A standard administrative unit
A role-assignable security group only
A dynamic Microsoft 365 group

questions.prompt: Several Azure virtual machines are rebuilt frequently but must keep the same least-privileged access to an Azure Storage account. The customer wants no application secret or certificate rotation. Which identity design best fits?
questions.explanation: A user-assigned managed identity has an independent lifecycle, can be attached to multiple Azure resources, and obtains tokens without stored application credentials. A system-assigned identity is tied to one resource and is deleted with it; an app registration using a secret or certificate reintroduces credential lifecycle work.

choices.text: One user-assigned managed identity attached to the virtual machines and granted the required storage role
A separate system-assigned managed identity whose object is copied to each rebuilt virtual machine
An application registration with a client secret stored in every virtual machine
A shared Microsoft Entra user account assigned the storage role
A certificate credential renewed manually for each virtual machine

questions.prompt: A customer's helpdesk leads already have an eligible Microsoft Entra role in PIM. Every activation must be approved by the service owner and must use the customer's phishing-resistant authentication strength. Choose two role-setting controls.
questions.explanation: PIM role settings can require approval and name the approvers, and they can require a Conditional Access authentication context whose policy enforces a selected authentication strength. Notifications provide awareness but not approval, while changing the eligible assignment to permanent active removes the activation gate entirely.
questions.sourceName: Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM

choices.text: Require approval to activate and configure the service owner as an approver
Require a Conditional Access authentication context that enforces the phishing-resistant authentication strength
Send an activation notification without requiring approval
Convert the eligible assignment to permanently active
Require a Microsoft Entra hybrid joined device instead of the stated authentication strength
Assign the service owner the role being activated rather than the approver responsibility

questions.prompt: A holding company keeps employees in a central Microsoft Entra tenant and needs selected users automatically created, updated, and deprovisioned as external users in several resource tenants. Manual invitations must be avoided. Which capability should the MSP configure?
questions.explanation: Cross-tenant synchronization provisions scoped users from a source tenant into target tenants and manages their lifecycle and mapped attributes. B2B invitations are suited to individual collaboration but do not provide the requested automatic provisioning lifecycle; entitlement management governs packages of access after identities are available.

choices.text: Microsoft Entra cross-tenant synchronization from the central source tenant
Manual Microsoft Entra B2B invitation redemption for every employee
Microsoft Entra Connect cloud sync between the resource tenants
An access package with no external-user provisioning process

questions.prompt: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access policies are enforced after first-factor authentication is completed.
questions.explanation: Conditional Access is a policy engine, not the first authentication factor. It evaluates signals and enforces access decisions after first-factor authentication.

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: A user with an eligible Microsoft Entra PIM role assignment continuously receives that role's permissions until the eligibility period expires.
questions.explanation: Eligibility permits the user to request activation. The privileged permissions are granted only while the role has an active assignment, not throughout the full eligibility period.

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: Publishing an on-premises application through Microsoft Entra application proxy requires opening inbound firewall ports so the application proxy service can initiate connections to the private network connector.
questions.explanation: Application proxy communication originates from the private network connector and is outbound only. The design does not require inbound firewall openings for the connector.

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: Cross-tenant synchronization is not a tenant-migration tool because synchronized users continue to authenticate against their source tenant.
questions.explanation: Cross-tenant synchronization provisions identities for collaboration in target tenants. The source identity remains authoritative for authentication, and the feature does not migrate the user's Microsoft 365 data.

choices.text: True
False
